document.source_title: US Senate refuses to push through ICE funding amid row over Trump’s ballroom
document.summary: The US Senate has decided not to pass a $70 billion funding bill for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) due to controversies surrounding a $1 billion proposal for security measures linked to Donald Trump's ballroom project. Republican senators expressed concerns about the potential backlash from voters regarding the use of taxpayer dollars for Trump's East Wing modernization project. Senate Democrats have pledged to oppose the funding and continue to challenge Republicans on this issue as the deadline approaches.
key_takeaways: Senate Republicans will not pass a $70 billion ICE funding bill due to controversies over Trump's ballroom project.
A $1 billion proposal for security measures tied to Trump's ballroom has caused significant backlash.
Concerns about alienating voters ahead of the midterm elections are influencing Republican senators' decisions.
Senate Democrats are actively opposing the funding for Trump's ballroom and plan to challenge Republicans.
Trump claims the ballroom funding is necessary for national security.
Polling indicates that most Americans oppose the controversial ballroom project.
document.why_it_matters: This situation highlights the ongoing tensions within the Republican Party regarding funding for Trump's projects and the potential impact on voter sentiment ahead of the midterm elections. It also reflects broader concerns about government spending and priorities in the context of rising living costs.

claims.claim_text: The US Senate will not pass the $70 billion legislation for ICE funding.
claims.evidence_text: The US Senate will not pass the $70bn legislation ahead of a 1 June deadline set by the US president.

claims.claim_text: Republican senators are concerned about the backlash from voters regarding Trump's ballroom funding.
claims.evidence_text: The plan prompted intense anxiety among congressional Republicans, who feared diverting taxpayer dollars toward Trump’s 'East Wing modernization project' would risk alienating voters.

claims.claim_text: Senate Democrats are opposing the funding for Trump's ballroom.
claims.evidence_text: Senate Democrats pledged to oppose the funding for Trump’s ballroom.

entities.evidence_text: The US Senate will not pass the $70bn legislation ahead of a 1 June deadline.

entities.name: Donald Trump

entities.evidence_text: Trump, meanwhile, continued to claim the ballroom funding proposal under consideration was for 'national security'.

entities.name: Chuck Schumer
entities.evidence_text: Addressing reporters on Capitol Hill on Thursday, Chuck Schumer accused Republicans of trying to 'sneak' ballroom funding into the bill.

entities.name: Bill Cassidy
entities.description: Republican senator who expressed concerns about the funding.
entities.evidence_text: I don’t actually see any legal precedent for that,” Republican senator Bill Cassidy told reporters.

events.event: Senate Republicans announce decision on ICE funding
events.evidence_text: Republican senators told reporters on Thursday, as lawmakers leave Washington for the Memorial Day recess.
